title: Shri Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga Temple, Ujjain (MP)
intro: The Shri Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga Temple, located in the city of Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, is one of the most famous and sacred temples in India dedicated to Lord Shiva.
description:
Shri Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga Temple – Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh
Introduction
The Shri Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga Temple, located in the city of Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, is one of the most famous and sacred temples in India dedicated to Lord Shiva. This temple is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas of Lord Shiva and is specifically known for the Mahakal (Lord of Time/Death) form of the deity.
Located in the holy city of Ujjain, this temple is world-famous for its unique Bhasma Aarti and ancient religious traditions. Every year, millions of devotees visit here to have darshan of Lord Mahakal.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga Temple
 Other Names: Mahakal Temple, Ujjain Mahakal Temple
 Location: Jayasinghpura, Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh 456006, India
 Presiding Deity: Lord Mahakaleshwar (Lord Shiva)
 Significance: One of the 12 major Jyotirlingas
 Nearest Major City: Indore (approx. 55 km)
History and Significance
The Mahakaleshwar Temple is one of the most important religious sites dedicated to Lord Shiva. Ujjain has been considered a city of spiritual and cultural significance since ancient times.
According to religious beliefs, Lord Shiva resides here in the Mahakal form, who is considered the Lord of time and death. The most famous tradition of this temple is the Bhasma Aarti, which is performed in the early morning as a special ritual of worship for Lord Mahakal.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air
 Devi Ahilyabai Holkar Airport, Indore: Distance from the temple is approx. 55 km. Taxi and bus services are available from Indore Airport to Ujjain.
🚆 By Train
 Ujjain Junction: Distance from the temple is approx. 2 km. Autos, e-rickshaws, and taxis are easily available from the railway station to the temple.
🛣️ By Road
Ujjain is well-connected by road to major cities like Indore, Bhopal, Ahmedabad, and others. You can reach here by bus, taxi, or private vehicle.
Best Time to Visit
The Ujjain Mahakal Temple remains open for devotees throughout the year.
Best time for the Yatra:
October to March.
Special Occasions:
 Mahashivratri
 Shravan Maas
 Nag Panchami
Temple Timings
🕒 4:00 AM to 11:00 PM
(Timings may change during special rituals and festivals.)
Famous Seva
Bhasma Aarti:
The Bhasma Aarti is one of the most famous religious traditions of the Mahakal Temple. This aarti is dedicated to Lord Mahakal in the early morning, following a specific and ancient ritual. Prior permission and registration may be required to attend the Bhasma Aarti.
Main Festivals
 Mahashivratri: A major festival for the special worship and celebration of Lord Shiva.
 Shravan Maas: Special worship of Lord Mahakal is performed during the holy month of Sawan.
 Nag Panchami: An important religious festival associated with Lord Shiva.
Nearby Attractions
 Harsiddhi Temple: A famous Shaktipeeth and religious site in Ujjain.
 Kaal Bhairav Temple: A famous temple dedicated to Lord Kaal Bhairav.
 Ram Ghat: A sacred ghat situated on the banks of the Shipra River.
 Sandipani Ashram: It is believed that Lord Shri Krishna received his education here.
Dress Code
 Wearing traditional and modest clothing is considered appropriate in the temple.
 Traditional attire is prioritized for special rituals and the Bhasma Aarti.
Facilities for Devotees
 Darshan arrangements
 Puja and Seva facilities
 Prasad facilities
 Security arrangements
 Help centers
 Nearby hotels and Dharmashalas (guesthouses)
Conclusion
The Shri Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga Temple is one of the holiest abodes of Lord Shiva. This temple in Ujjain is famous worldwide for its ancient traditions, the Bhasma Aarti, and its spiritual atmosphere. For devotees, darshan of Mahakal is considered a special opportunity for faith, peace, and a spiritual experience.

faq.q1: To which deity is the Mahakaleshwar Temple dedicated?
faq.a1: This temple is dedicated to the Mahakal form of Lord Shiva and is one of the 12 Jyotirlingas.
faq.q2: Where is the Mahakaleshwar Temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh.
faq.q3: Which is the most famous ritual at the Mahakal Temple?
faq.a3: The Bhasma Aarti is the most famous ritual at the Mahakal Temple.
faq.q4: Which is the nearest railway station to the Mahakaleshwar Temple?
faq.q5: What is the best time to visit the Mahakal Temple?
faq.a5:
The period from October to March is considered ideal for the pilgrimage.
